items.title: RON95, RON97 and diesel prices in Peninsular Malaysia lowered for May 14–20 period, says Finance Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Retail prices for RON97, RON95 without subsidy and diesel in Peninsular Malaysia will be lower for the May 14 to 20 period, with RON97 set at RM4.70 per litre, RON95 (without subsidy) at RM3.87 per litre, and diesel at RM4.87 per litre, the Ministry of Finance said. Under the revised pricing, RON97 is down 20 sen from RM4.90 per litre, while RON95 without subsidy is down 15 sen from RM4.02 per litre, and diesel in Peninsular Malaysia is down 30 sen from RM5.17 per litre. The government maintained subsidised prices for targeted groups, with RON95 under Budi95 remaining at RM1.99 per litre, diesel in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan at RM2.15 per litre, and diesel under SKPS and SKDS at RM2.05 and RM2.15 per litre respectively.

items.title: Police strengthen convoy SOPs following fatal FRU crash, says IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: One year after the fatal Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) crash that sparked nationwide scrutiny over police operational safety and welfare standards, police convoy standards have been improved with upgraded logistics and strengthened protection measures for personnel. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the improvements followed continuous internal reviews, particularly involving large-scale convoy movements and deployments. Khalid said the department was also continuously upgrading operational facilities and transportation assets to improve personnel safety and efficiency.

items.title: Evolving drone war in southern Lebanon clouds Iran peace prospects
items.summary: In recent weeks, Hizbollah has used cheap, easy-to-assemble First Person View kamikaze drones to transform the war it has been fighting since it began firing on Israel on March 2, days after the US-Israeli forces began their attacks on Iran. The Iran-backed group has published videos of more than 45 FPV attacks, 28 of them in the nearly four weeks since the ceasefire, which had halted Israeli attacks on the Lebanese capital before Israel said it targeted a Hizbollah commander there on Thursday. Israel is firing back, with at least two deadly FPV drone attacks against Hizbollah in April complete with published drone images purporting to show Hizbollah fighters up close.

items.title: M'sian taxi drivers, tricked into smuggling contraband into Singapore
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A 71-year-old Malaysian taxi driver was detained during a routine checkpoint inspection in Singapore's Woodlands Checkpoint on April 3. The elderly driver and another passenger, 73, were also detained. A Johor Baru-based taxi driver, Siew Toh, 56, who frequently ferries passengers across the Causeway, said drivers are increasingly exposed to risk but have limited legal ability to intervene.

items.title: IGP: Cybercrime, online scams and cross-border threats need united response
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Modern security threats such as cybercrime, online scams and increasingly complex cross-border crimes require strategic cooperation among various parties to be addressed effectively. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said losses due to online scams in Malaysia reached RM2.7 billion last year, highlighting that current security challenges cannot be handled by enforcement agencies alone. “The theme ‘Synergised Policing: Securing the Future Together’ highlighted during the PDRM Special Dialogue V 2026 (PSD V 2026) programme is highly relevant to today’s global security realities.

items.title: Police say Jho Low probe ongoing despite pardon reports, says IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police investigations into fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, also known as Jho Low, are continuing despite reports that he sought a pardon from United States President Donald Trump. "This Jho Low issue is still an ongoing investigation. Low remains wanted by authorities in several countries over his alleged role in the 1MDB scandal.

items.title: MACC denies blocking reports, says anti-graft agency open to all complaints
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) does not prevent any individual from lodging reports and remains open to receiving information related to corruption from any party, regardless of the individual’s position or background. The MACC stressed that allegations claiming the commission had prevented any individual from making a report were untrue. “The MACC is always open to receiving reports and information related to corruption from any party, regardless of the position or background of the individual involved.

items.title: Jho Low still under probe despite Trump pardon bid claims, says IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Police are continuing investigations into fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, said Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail. He said the case remained under police attention and action despite reports claiming that Jho Low was seeking a pardon from United States President Donald Trump. Mohd Khalid said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) was also maintaining good relations with the relevant countries in efforts to continue investigations into the case.

items.title: 70 children killed in West Bank, East Jerusalem since 2025: Unicef
items.summary: GENEVA: Seventy children have been killed in the occupied Palestinian territories excluding Gaza since early 2025, amounting to around one a week, while over 800 have been injured, the UN children's agency said on Tuesday. Most of those killed or injured in the West Bank and East Jerusalem were hit by live ammunition but others were stabbed, beaten or pepper-sprayed, Unicef said. He said 93 per cent of the children who had lost their lives since January 2025 had been killed by Israeli forces.

items.title: Sungai Petani cops probe claim 13-day-old baby suffered fractured thigh during jaundice treatment
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, May 13 — Police have confirmed receiving a report regarding allegations that a 13-day-old baby boy suffered a fractured thigh bone while receiving treatment for jaundice at a hospital in this district recently. Kuala Muda police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said a police report was lodged by the baby’s father last Monday and investigations had since been initiated. The incident later went viral on social media, including photographs showing the baby receiving treatment at the hospital.

items.title: 38 accident cases linked to drink-driving reported in Penang since 2021
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang recorded a total of 38 road accidents involving drivers under the influence of alcohol between 2021 and April this year. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the state recorded six cases in 2021, which declined to five cases in 2022, before rising again to 13 cases in 2023 and 14 cases as of April this year. Chow said various enforcement measures have been implemented to curb drunk-driving incidents, including regular operations against driving under the influence at high-risk accident zones during nighttime hours and at entertainment hotspots.

items.title: After Teluk Intan FRU crash, police tighten SOPs for large convoy operations, says IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) has tightened its standard operating procedures (SOPs) for large-scale convoy assignments to prevent a recurrence of the fatal accident involving a Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) truck in Teluk Intan, Perak, last year. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the improvements place particular emphasis on compliance with SOPs by all personnel while carrying out their duties. In addition, Mohd Khalid said the PDRM is also continuously upgrading FRU logistics and equipment through procuring new vehicles funded by government allocations.

items.title: Israel strikes kill 8 people in south Beirut, including 2 children
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israeli strikes targeting cars on a highway south of Beirut on Wednesday killed eight people, including two children, Lebanon's health ministry said, in the latest attacks despite a ceasefire in the Israel-Hizbollah war. Earlier, the Israeli military said it launched a new wave of strikes across southern Lebanon Wednesday, targeting Hizbollah infrastructure, despite a truce with the Lebanese government to halt fighting with the armed group. On Tuesday, Israeli strikes killed 13 people in southern Lebanon including a soldier, a child and two rescue workers, the health ministry said.

items.title: Victims claim up to RM133k lost in alleged bogus aesthetic training scam
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Victims claim they lost up to RM133,000 after enrolling in an alleged fake aesthetic training programme involving forged certifications, improper medical procedures and false credentials. He said the programme misused academic titles and institutions, including purported links to Universiti Malaya qualifications and master's degrees. She said she never received proper certification despite making payments for nearly two years.

items.title: Police bust illegal e-waste factories in Negri Sembilan, seize RM47mil in items
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Police have busted illegal electronic waste (e-waste) processing operations and seized items worth more than RM47 million following raids on three factories over the past two months. Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the raids, conducted under Ops Waste, were the result of continuous intelligence gathering. He said one raid was carried out at an industrial site in Nilai on April 15, while two others were conducted in Kuala Sawah on May 6.

items.title: MACC denies claims of barring public from filing complaints
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has denied claims that it bars people from filing complaints, saying the allegations are false and aimed at tarnishing its image. The MACC said it welcomes complaints from anyone, regardless of their position or background, but all visitors must comply with standard security procedures. It said its premises fall under the Protected Areas and Protected Places Act 1959, meaning only authorised individuals may enter and all visitors are subject to security checks.

items.title: Japan 'robot wolves' in high demand to scare off bears
items.summary: TOKYO: A Japanese company making ferocious-looking robot wolves is being swamped by orders after record numbers of fatal bear attacks on humans last year. "Monster Wolf" is an animatoronic scarecrow with flashing red eyes that howls and growls menacingly to scare away wild animals. Ohta Seiki, the Hokkaido-based firm that makes the devices, has already received around 50 orders this year, more than the usual volume for an entire year.

items.title: New MACC chief vows tougher crackdown on corruption, abuse of power
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — Newly appointed Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman has vowed to intensify efforts to combat corruption, abuse of power and malfeasance, while pledging stronger enforcement, greater transparency and improved public trust in the agency. Abd Halim said restoring public confidence in the MACC was an urgent priority, stressing that the commission must remain firm, transparent and accountable while being fully guided by the rule of law, according to national news agency Bernama. Among the reforms proposed were tighter internal coordination, enhanced operational efficiency, strengthened recruitment of investigation officers and continued public education efforts on corruption prevention.

items.title: Kepong restaurant assault victim fears for safety after unprovoked attack
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The victim of a brutal assault at a Bak Kut Teh restaurant in Jalan Besar Kepong last week is now fearing for his life, and that of his family, following reports that his assailants could be linked to secret societies. The man, who requested anonymity due to safety concerns, said he has been left traumatised and continues to suffer physically and emotionally from the incident which he described as totally unprovoked. The victim, an entrepreneur and company director, said he now struggles with sleepless nights and no longer dares to dine out alone.

items.title: New MACC chief vows tougher, independent anti-graft drive rooted in rule of law
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Newly appointed Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman has pledged to steer the anti-graft agency with a firmer and more independent approach grounded in the rule of law, while vowing there would be no compromise against corruption and abuse of power. In his first official statement after assuming office today, Abd Halim said the commission would act without fear or favour against any individual who misused their position or influence. He said the MACC under his leadership would enforce the law with greater firmness, fairness and professionalism, regardless of rank, position or influence.

items.title: Besieged Starmer seeks to heal Labour divisions in King's Speech
items.summary: LONDON: King Charles III will on Wednesday outline UK leader Keir Starmer's legislative plans, during a pomp-filled ceremony that could have huge repercussions for the embattled prime minister's future. It is the latest crunch moment in Starmer's 22-month premiership and comes after Labour descended into open warfare over the prime minister's fate following the party's heavy defeats in local and regional elections. On Wednesday, health minister Wes Streeting met Starmer at Downing Street, as speculation swirled over whether he could launch a leadership bid.

items.title: 21-year-old university student among three injured in express bus crash
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A 21-year-old private university student was among three people injured after an express bus crashed into a road milling machine at Km102.4 of the North-South Expressway (northbound) near Yong Peng early today. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Shahrulanuar Mushaddat Abdullah Sani said the student suffered injuries to her mouth. Apart from the student, also injured during the accident were the 42-year-old bus driver and another bus passenger, 56, who suffered head injuries.

items.title: Petronas delivers Malaysia's first sustainable aviation fuel to KLIA
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) has delivered Malaysia's first blended sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) to Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA), marking a key milestone in the country's push towards lower-carbon aviation. Locally blended at Petronas' blending facilities, the International Sustainability & Carbon Certification-certified SAF was delivered to KLIA via Malaysian Refining Company's multi-product pipeline. The fuel delivery was made for Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG).

items.title: Penang has seven of 17 licensed e-waste firms, strong base to drive high-tech green economy says Bertam rep
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 13 — Penang has significant potential to turn waste into new economic wealth by attracting high-technology foreign investments as a new driver of the state economy. Datuk Seri Reezal Merican Naina Merican (BN-Bertam) said the state government should therefore formulate a comprehensive green waste and high-technology e-waste master plan. Reezal Merican, who is also chairman of Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation, also stressed the importance of waste-to-energy (WTE) projects, which could help resolve solid waste issues while generating energy.

items.title: Rafizi, Nik Nazmi to announce new political direction this Sunday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pandan member of Parliament Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Setiawangsa MP Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad will announce their new political direction on Sunday. Rafizi, who is the former economy minister, said the past year had given both of them the space to weigh various views, considerations and factors before deciding on their next political path. "The past year has given us both the opportunity to take into account various views, considerations and factors in deciding our next political direction," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Commission steps up engagement ahead of consumer credit act implementation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Consumer Credit Commission (CCC) is stepping up engagement with industry players ahead of the implementation of the Consumer Credit Act 2025 (CCA), which will bring non-bank credit and credit service providers under a formal regulatory framework. The commission said the authorisation framework, covering licensing and registration, marks a significant milestone in strengthening oversight of the consumer credit industry. Under Phase 1 of the CCA implementation, six business activities will fall within the commission's regulatory scope.

items.title: Cleaner pleads guilty to sexually assaulting two teens at rehab centre
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: A cleaner pleaded guilty at the Sessions Court today to two counts of physical sexual assault against two teenage boys at a Social Welfare Department rehabilitation centre. According to the first charge, he committed physical sexual assault against a 14-year-old boy in a toilet next to a vocational workshop at the Seberang Prai Utara institution at 11am on April 21. Both charges were framed under Section 14(b) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.

items.title: Pahang seeks RM500 million federal funding for Gebeng halal hub development
items.summary: KUANTAN: Pahang will submit an application to secure an allocation of RM500 million to the federal government for the construction of the Gebeng Halal Hub here. State Investment, Industry, Science, Technology and Innovation Committee chairman Datuk Mohd Nizar Mohamad Najib said the funds are intended for infrastructure development in an area covering 295 hectares. He said the targeted industries for the Gebeng Halal Hub include halal and non-halal food manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, agricultural-and meat-based products, as well as halal logistics and related services.

items.title: [UPDATED] Najib obtains stay of execution on US$1.18bil SRC judgment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Najib Razak today obtained a stay of execution on a High Court judgment ordering him to pay US$1.18 billion pending his appeal over losses suffered by SRC International Sdn Bhd. Judge Datuk Ahmad Fairuz Zainol Abidin ruled that special circumstances justified the stay, citing the unprecedented magnitude of the judgment sum. Ahmad Fairuz said enforcing the judgment immediately could cause irreparable prejudice to the former prime minister, while SRC's entitlement to the judgment sum would only be deferred pending the appeal.

items.title: Malaysia hits 10.64 million international arrivals in Q1 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia recorded a new tourism milestone in the first quarter of this year as international arrivals rose 5.4 per cent to 10.64 million visitors, Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing said. Tiong said the latest figures marked the second consecutive year the country exceeded 10 million international arrivals in the first quarter, surpassing even pre-pandemic levels. He said this year's growth was driven largely by the Chinese New Year holidays, which helped Malaysia set a new monthly record in February.

items.title: Malaysia ‘very disappointed’ as Norway blocks missile export permit for LCS deal, says Fahmi
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — Malaysia is “very disappointed” with the Norwegian government after it allegedly refused to issue an export permit for missiles already contracted by Malaysia, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil said today. “The Malaysian government is very disappointed with the actions of the Norwegian government, which did not approve the export permit for the delivery of missiles,” he said after the weekly Cabinet meeting. “The Malaysian government cannot accept the excuses given by the Norwegian government.

items.title: Baling mechanic claims trial to illegal possession of firearms
items.summary: Mohd Shahifuldin Mohd Shahiran, 46, pleaded not guilty to all charges read before Sessions Court Judge Rohaida Ishak, who also sat as magistrate. According to the first and second charges, he was accused of possessing a Colt UTC/TIC .45 calibre pistol and a magazine, as well as a Brazil-made Forjas Taurus S.A PT 24/7 9-millimetre pistol and a magazine without a valid licence or permit. For the third charge, Mohd Shahifuldin was accused of possessing a black imitation pistol made in Taiwan without lawful reason at the same date, time and place.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan leadership dispute: Court sets July 28 hearing on Dewan Keadilan dan Undang jurisdiction challenge
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 13 — The High Court today ordered the defendants in an originating summons filed by Datuk Mubarak Thahak and five others to file affidavits within seven days in support of their challenge to the court’s jurisdiction. Justice Roz Mawar Rozain issued the order after hearing preliminary objections raised by the first defendant, Negeri Sembilan Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) secretary Raja Norazli Raja Nordin, and the second defendant, the DKU. Counsel for both defendants, Steven Thiru, objected to the originating summons on the ground that the court lacks jurisdiction under Article 16(3) of the Negeri Sembilan State Constitution 1959.

items.title: Madani Mediation Centre to launch May 18, offers free dispute resolution for claims under RM250,000
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — The government’s new Madani Mediation Centre initiative will be officially launched on May 18 in Kuala Lumpur, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil announced today. Fahmi said the initiative falls under the Prime Minister’s Department and is overseen by Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said. “In general, Mediasi Madani is a pro bono initiative involving commercial mediation,” he said after the weekly Cabinet meeting.

items.title: Italy tests two people for hantavirus amid quarantine measures
items.summary: ROME: Italy is testing biological samples from two people for possible hantavirus infection, an Argentine tourist hospitalised with pneumonia and a man from Calabria in voluntary isolation, the Health Ministry said on Tuesday. The Argentine tourist had left an endemic area back home on April 30 and traveled to Italy on a Buenos Aires-Rome flight before later going to Sicily, where she was hospitalised with pneumonia, the ministry said in a statement. BRITISH TOURIST IN QUARANTINE In a separate development, a British tourist was located in Milan and placed in quarantine after British authorities warned he had been on the same flight as the Dutch woman, the Health Ministry said.

items.title: Cops cut power to businesses fronting illegal gambling dens
items.summary: KLUANG: Police cut electricity supply to six premises suspected of operating illegal public lottery and online gambling activities during an integrated operation here yesterday. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Bahrin Mohd Noh said the premises were identified as operating illegal public lottery outlets and online gambling activities through top-up systems within the district. Bahrin said similar integrated operations would continue to ensure premises involved in illegal gambling activities were brought to book.

items.title: Malaysia's MRO sector on track to RM55bil target
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) industry is on track to achieve its RM55 billion revenue target by end-2030 driven by rising aircraft demand across Asia Pacific, increasing aerospace investments and growing local engineering capabilities. He said the aerospace industry has evolved into a major economic growth sector under the National Industrial Master Plan 2030 and the Malaysia Aerospace Industry Blueprint 2030. "In the early 2000s, Malaysia's aerospace industry was still developing.

items.title: Kota Marudu house fire claims life of disabled teen boy, 28-year-old brother hurt in rescue attempt
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 13 — A teenage boy with disabilities died while his elder brother was injured in a house fire at Kampung Torintidon in Kota Marudu early this morning. Kota Marudu Fire and Rescue Station chief Debli Mamoi said they received an emergency call regarding the incident at 3.23 am. Debli said firefighters found the charred remains of the 16-year-old boy while his 28-year-old brother, who sustained burn injuries, had been sent to Kota Marudu Hospital by villagers.

items.title: Bank of America relocates to Merdeka 118
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank of America has officially inaugurated its new office on the 38th floor of Merdeka 118, marking another milestone in its long-standing presence in Malaysia since first establishing operations in the country in 1959. Now in its 67th year in Malaysia, Bank of America employs more than 90 staff locally and offers a full suite of financial services spanning corporate banking, investment banking, global markets and treasury solutions. The office opening comes amid the bank's expanding involvement in Malaysia's broader economic and investment initiatives.

items.title: Azam denies dealings with Albert Tei, says ready to face probe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has denied allegations made against him by businessman Albert Tei. Responding to Tei's latest claims, Azam said Tei was free to lodge any formal complaint with the relevant enforcement agencies and said that he is prepared to cooperate with any investigation. Earlier today, Tei claimed he was prevented from entering the MACC headquarters here when he attempted to lodge a report related to Azam.

items.title: Trump vows to push Xi to 'open up' China at superpower summit [WATCH]
items.summary: BEIJING: US President Donald Trump said he would ask Xi Jinping to "open up" China to American firms as he headed to Beijing on Wednesday for a high-stakes summit that will also bring up the Iran war. Trump wrote on social media after departing Washington. As he departed the White House, Trump said he expected a "long talk" with Xi about Iran, which sells most of its US-sanctioned oil to China.

items.title: [UPDATED] PM directs ministries to engage hawkers over low aid participation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has instructed relevant ministries to engage hawkers following findings that participation in formal financial assistance programmes among the group remains very low. "During today's cabinet meeting, the prime minister raised the findings of a study which showed that participation among hawkers in formal financial assistance programmes remains very low. "Based on the study, only five per cent of hawkers received assistance.

items.title: [UPDATED] Fahmi: Jho Low pardon to be raised at cabinet meeting next week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The cabinet is expected to discuss next week reports that fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, or Jho Low, has sought a pardon from United States President Donald Trump. Unity government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the matter was not discussed during today's cabinet meeting. Fahmi was responding to a question on whether the Malaysian government planned to make formal representations to the United States authorities opposing any possible pardon for Jho Low.

items.title: Communications minister clarifies report on BUDI95 cuts, says deputy finance minister misquoted
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — The Cabinet has not discussed any proposal to cut the BUDI95 fuel subsidy quota, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil said today. He added that earlier reports quoting Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong as saying the government is looking to reduce the monthly BUDI95 allocation were inaccurate. “The Ministry of Economy studied the levels of 300 litres, 200 litres, 150 litres and 100 litres to see how many Malaysians fall within those categories,” he said.

items.title: Fahmi: Cabinet yet to discuss Jho Low’s US pardon bid, maybe next week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — The Cabinet today did not discuss reports that fugitive Malaysian Low Taek Jho has sought a pardon from the United States President Donald Trump, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil said. The wanted businessman better known as Jho Low was reported by the Wall Street Journal (WSJ) to have filed for a pardon from Trump ‌weeks ago, citing ⁠people familiar ⁠with the ⁠matter. If ⁠granted, the US ⁠would purportedly remove criminal charges against Low.

items.title: All 104 World Cup 2026 matches to be shown on RTM, RTMKlik and Unifi TV, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Malaysians will be able to watch all 104 matches of the 2026 Fifa World Cup through RTM and Unifi TV platforms, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil confirmed today. Speaking after the weekly Cabinet meeting, Fahmi said all matches from the expanded 48-team tournament would be available through RTM channels and streaming platform RTMKlik, alongside Unifi TV. “In line with the announcement I made last week, I would like to announce that all 104 matches will be broadcast on RTM and Unifi TV,” he said today during his weekly briefing.

items.title: Shah Alam police confirm wanted man in Bukit Kemuning teen assault case has history of rape, robbery and holding another captive
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 13 — A man being sought by police for allegedly confining and sexually assaulting a 14-year-old boy at an apartment in Bukit Kemuning, Section 32 here, has a previous criminal record involving rape, robbery and wrongfully confining a woman about 18 years ago. Shah Alam district police chief ACP Ramsay Embol confirmed that the suspect had been convicted of the offences and jailed in 2008. He said victim’s hands and feet were tied before he was allegedly sexually assaulted by the suspect.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of afternoon rough weather as thunderstorms sweep across parts of peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds affecting eight states until 5pm today. In a statement today, MetMalaysia said the adverse weather conditions are expected in Perlis, Kedah (Langkawi) and Perak, involving Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim. Similar conditions are also forecast in Pahang, specifically Cameron Highlands and Bentong, as well as Hulu Selangor in Selangor.

items.title: CCTV, phone videos lead to jail for repo man behind Johor Bahru paint-splash attacks
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 13 — A 28-year-old car repossessor was sentenced to a total of seven months in jail by two magistrates today after pleading guilty to splashing red paint on two houses in the city in separate incidents last year and in April. Muhamad Yaccob admitted to splashing red paint on a house in Taman Mount Austin on April 7 this year and on a condominium unit in Twin Danga Residensi on December 24 last year. Court documents showed CCTV footage captured Muhamad Yaccob arriving at the homes before splashing paint and fleeing.

items.title: Serial molester who targeted women on LRT, MRT gets longer jail term, caning despite plea to return to Bangladesh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — A foreign construction worker was sentenced to 18 months in prison and one stroke of the cane by the High Court here today for outraging the modesty of five women near LRT and MRT stations in the capital last month. Justice Datuk Aslam Zainuddin imposed the sentence on Md Romjan Ali, a 41-year-old Bangladeshi national, after reviewing the May 6 decisions of four Magistrates’ Courts, which had sentenced him to six months’ imprisonment and RM14,500 fine. “For all five cases, the court will impose a global sentence.

items.title: No alcohol, please: Wine consumption slides in 2025 due to tariffs, fewer younger consumers
items.summary: PARIS, May 13 — Wine consumption fell worldwide last year, the industry’s trade body said Tuesday, amid changing lifestyles and economic pressure on consumers. It found consumption slid by 2.7 per cent last year to 208 million hectolitres, taking the cumulative drop since 2018 to 14 per cent. In China, wine consumption fell by 13 per cent last year and by 61 per cent since 2020.

items.title: New MACC chief Abdul Halim starts first day in office as Azam Baki exits after 42 years
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman officially began his duties today as the new chief commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). In a Facebook post, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar also congratulated Abdul Halim on his appointment. Shamsul Azri also shared photographs of Abdul Halim receiving his appointment letter as the new MACC chief commissioner.

items.title: Gua Musang Form One student dies after collapsing at hostel
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 13 — A male Form One student died after reportedly being found unconscious at the hostel of Sekolah Menengah Paloh in Gua Musang yesterday. Gua Musang police chief Supt Sik Choon Foo said police received a report regarding the incident from the victim’s father at 8.53 am yesterday. Sik added that further police examination found no elements of crime in the incident.

items.title: Three more bodies found in Pulau Pangkor migrant boat tragedy
items.summary: LUMUT, May 13 — Three more bodies of women, believed to be victims of a boat capsize incident involving undocumented Indonesian migrants off Pulau Pangkor on Monday, have been found. Marine Police Region One Operations Command officer DSP Mazre Che Mahmod said the three bodies were discovered by Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) vessel KD Sri Indera Sakti about 20 nautical miles from the location of the incident at around 6 pm yesterday. The third day of the SAR operation resumed at 7 am today, involving the Marine Police, RMN and the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA),” he said.

items.title: Maritime industry leader warns bill of lading mismatches can expose shipping sector to major risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — A senior maritime industry executive has warned that mismatches involving bills of lading (BL) and physical cargo movement are emerging as a growing operational and regulatory risk in the global shipping and commodity trading sector. The shipping industry veteran, who has more than 30 years of experience, said the BL is closely tied to banking transactions, insurance arrangements, Customs declarations, cargo ownership representation and sanctions compliance. “The biggest risk for a shipping agent is when the physical cargo movement and the documentation stop aligning.

items.title: Federal Court adjourns Isa Samad’s bid to review reinstated corruption conviction
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — The Federal Court today adjourned the hearing of former Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) chairman Tan Sri Mohd Isa Abdul Samad’s application seeking leave to review a previous Federal Court ruling that reinstated his corruption conviction, six-year prison sentence and RM15.45 million fine. Chief Judge of Malaya Datuk Seri Hashim Hamzah, who chaired a three-member bench, adjourned the hearing scheduled today after allowing the defence’s request to view the court recordings and transcription (CRT) of the open court proceedings held on Feb 5 and 10 this year. Also sitting on the bench were Chief Judge of Sabah and Sarawak Datuk Azizah Nawawi and Federal Court judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah.

items.title: Roofs torn off, trees uprooted, and power cut as whirlwind batters MPL Housing in Labuan, one injured
items.summary: LABUAN, May 13 — A residential area at MPL Housing near Tanjung Aru here was hit by a tornado-like storm last night, damaging 33 houses and leaving one man injured. The incident, which occurred at about 9.40 pm, saw strong winds ripping off rooftops, damaging several vehicles and uprooting large trees in nearby areas. Several roads were blocked by scattered debris and fallen objects.

items.title: New routes along Pan Borneo highway to open from July, easing traffic around Kota Kinabalu
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 13 — Nine work packages under Phase 1A of the Sabah Pan Borneo Highway project are expected to be fully completed and opened to motorists this year, Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan said. “Previously, four out of the 15 work packages under Phase 1A were completed in stages between 2023 and 2025. Phase 1 of the Sabah Pan Borneo Highway project comprises 35 work packages covering a total alignment of 706km from Sindumin to Tawau.

items.title: Pahang set to gazette 524 hectares in Bera for permanent forest reserves
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 13 — The Pahang government has identified several forest areas to be proposed as new Permanent Forest Reserves (HSK) to balance development needs with environmental conservation, the State Assembly was told today. “The total area of Pahang is 3.60 million hectares, while the size of the Permanent Forest Reserves currently stands at 1.589 million hectares, representing 44.13 per cent of the state’s total land area. Wan Rosdy said the state government would continue ensuring that any HSK area degazetted for economic development purposes would be replaced with another forest area of equivalent value.

items.title: 1MDB task force chief says Jho Low should not be pardoned amid reported US clemency bid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Fugitive Malaysian financier Low Taek ‌Jho, a central figure in the multibillion-dollar scandal at state fund 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB), should not be pardoned, the chairman of the 1MDB taskforce said today, after a report said Low ‌was seeking clemency from US President Donald Trump. Low, widely known as Jho Low, faces multiple charges, including corruption and money laundering in the United States and Malaysia, for the key role he allegedly played in the misappropriation of at least US$4.5 billion from 1MDB. A White House official said Low’s request was not currently on the White House’s radar, the report said.
